sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Earnings Flash (AUB) Atlantic Union Bankshares Corporation Posts Q4 Adjusted EPS $0.97 per Share, vs. FactSet Est of $0.86
feed.summary: Atlantic Union Bankshares Corporation (AUB) reported adjusted earnings per share of $0.97 for the fourth quarter, surpassing FactSet's estimate of $0.86. The company's Q4 revenue also exceeded analyst expectations, reaching $387.2 million against an estimate of $379.0 million. This positive earnings flash indicates strong performance for the financial institution.

feed.title: Southern Missouri Bancorp Posts Strong Q2 Results, New Buyback
feed.summary: Southern Missouri Bancorp (SMBC) reported a strong Q2 2026, with net income up 23.9% year-on-year to $18.2 million and diluted EPS of $1.62. The bank also declared its 127th consecutive quarterly dividend of $0.25 per share and authorized a new share repurchase program for up to 550,000 shares, signaling confidence in its financial strength. Analysts currently rate SMBC stock as a Buy with a $70.00 price target, and TipRanks' AI Analyst, Spark, also provides an "Outperform" signal due to strong financial and technical indicators.

Despite this, insiders still own a significant 0.5% of the company, valued at US$1.4b, and the company is reporting growing earnings.

feed.title: Musinsa emerges as dark horse in race for Hoka distribution rights
feed.summary: Musinsa, a leading Korean fashion platform, has entered the competition for the domestic distribution rights of the popular running shoe brand Hoka. This move positions Musinsa as a strong contender against larger fashion conglomerates, leveraging its marketing and branding expertise. The battle for Hoka's distribution highlights the brand's rapid growth in Korea and its appeal as a premium sports brand in a cooling luxury market.

feed.title: 3 Top Growth Stocks With Significant Insider Ownership
feed.summary: This article identifies three growth stocks—AAON, Estée Lauder Companies, and On Holding—with significant insider ownership, suggesting strong confidence from company insiders despite recent market volatility. It details each company's financial performance, growth forecasts, and strategic initiatives, while also noting that some of these stocks might be overvalued according to Simply Wall St's valuation reports. The piece emphasizes the importance of insider ownership as an indicator of a company's future prospects and alignment with shareholder interests.

feed.title: Advanced Micro Devices Stock Jumps 8% As Trump’s Davos Remarks Calm Markets
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) stock surged 8% due to positive market reactions to President Trump's calming remarks at Davos, significant AI infrastructure wins, and fresh analyst upgrades. Investors are increasingly confident in AMD's competitive stance against rivals like Nvidia and Intel, propelled by strong data center growth, new AI-enabled PC product launches, and key partnerships with companies like OpenAI and Oracle. This reflects a growing belief in AMD's long-term earning potential, with attention now turning to Q4 2025 earnings.

feed.title: ADM's Premix Portfolio
feed.summary: ADM offers custom blending services and a range of specialty ingredients for livestock producers and pet food manufacturers in North America. Their premix portfolio includes tailor-made vitamin, mineral, and dry ingredient blends for both livestock and pet nutrition. ADM leverages its extensive research, manufacturing, and logistics capabilities to provide solution-based ingredients and quality products.

feed.title: Bear of the Day: Lamb Weston (LW)
feed.summary: Lamb Weston (LW), a global manufacturer of frozen potato products, has been designated a Zacks Rank #5 (Strong Sell) due to analysts' bearish outlook on its earnings. Despite exceeding consensus expectations in its latest quarter, the company's shares plunged, with sales growth remaining weak. The article highlights the importance of focusing on stocks with a Zacks Rank #1 or #2 for stronger earnings outlook and potential gains.

feed.title: Arctic Wolf detects surge in automated Fortinet FortiGate Firewall configuration attacks
feed.summary: Arctic Wolf has identified a new wave of automated attacks targeting Fortinet FortiGate devices since January 15, 2026. These attacks involve unauthorized firewall configuration changes, including creating generic persistence accounts, enabling VPN access, and exfiltrating firewall configurations. This activity mirrors a December 2025 campaign that exploited critical Fortinet authentication bypass flaws (CVE-2025-59718 and CVE-2025-59719) for malicious SSO logins and configuration theft.

feed.title: FDA clearances and approvals: Boston Scientific, BD, Elekta
feed.summary: The article reports on recent FDA clearances and approvals granted to medical device companies Boston Scientific, BD, and Elekta. It highlights these regulatory milestones as significant developments for the respective companies within the medical technology sector.

feed.title: The Next Frontier of Runtime Assembly Attacks: Leveraging LLMs to Generate Phishing JavaScript in Real Time
feed.summary: This article identifies a new frontier in web attacks where attackers use Large Language Models (LLMs) to dynamically generate and execute polymorphic phishing JavaScript in real time within a victim's browser. This AI-augmented runtime assembly technique is highly evasive, as the malicious code is delivered from trusted LLM domains and changes with each visit, making traditional network analysis difficult. The most effective defense involves runtime behavioral analysis within the browser, along with restricting unsanctioned LLM use and enhancing LLM safety guardrails.

feed.title: Ark Increases WeRide Holdings by Nearly 700,000 Shares, Rebuilding Chinese Tech Exposure
feed.summary: Ark Investment Management has significantly increased its holdings in WeRide, accumulating nearly 700,000 shares over three trading days, signaling a renewed interest in Chinese tech stocks after the 2021 regulatory crackdown. This move is complemented by an increased stake in Baidu and is supported by analysts who are optimistic about WeRide's global expansion plans, including a "Buy" rating from Citi with a price target implying significant upside. WeRide, specializing in autonomous driving technology, operates over 1,000 autonomous taxis and plans further international growth, with potential financing rounds and stock connect entries expected to boost its valuation and market sentiment.

feed.title: Form 424B2 CITIGROUP INC
feed.summary: Citigroup Global Markets Holdings Inc. is offering Callable Contingent Coupon Equity Linked Securities due February 1, 2029, linked to the performance of the Nasdaq-100 Index, Russell 2000 Index, and S&P 500 Index. These unsecured debt securities offer potential periodic contingent coupon payments, but investors face risks including potential loss of principal, no coupon payments if underlying values fall below barriers, and limited liquidity as the securities will not be listed on any exchange. The securities are subject to the credit risk of Citigroup Global Markets Holdings Inc. and Citigroup Inc.

feed.title: The Truth About State Street Corp.: Is This ‘Boring’ Stock Your Sneaky Power Move?
feed.summary: State Street Corp. (STT) is primarily an asset management and custody company, handling assets for large funds and institutions. The article explores whether this "boring" stock, which lacks viral appeal, could be a smart investment for those seeking stability and dividends rather than high-growth momentum. It details State Street's business model, how market conditions and interest rates affect it, and its competitive landscape, concluding that it's a "slow-burn" asset for long-term investors.

feed.title: Microsoft Wins $170 Million U.S. Air Force Cloud Contract for Azure Services
feed.summary: Microsoft has secured a $170.4 million firm-fixed-price task order from the U.S. Air Force to provide Azure cloud services for its Cloud One program. This non-competitive award, running through December 2028, supports military users and reinforces Microsoft's presence in defense cloud computing. The contract comes as Microsoft's stock has seen a 20% decline from its October peak, with analysts lowering price targets despite maintaining "buy" ratings.

feed.title: United States Power Rental Market Outlook 2025: Growth Trends,
feed.summary: DataM Intelligence has released a new research report on the "Power Rental Market Size 2025," offering insights into growth trends, industry drivers, and the competitive landscape for the U.S. power rental market. The report details the market's segmentation by fuel type, equipment, application, end-user, and power rating, highlighting strong demand from sectors like oil & gas, utilities, and data centers. Key players in this market include Aggreko, United Rentals, Caterpillar, Cummins, and Herc Holdings, all operating within a dynamic environment shaped by infrastructure projects, grid instability, and natural disasters.

feed.title: Kimberly-Clark Faces Investor Scrutiny Ahead of Key Earnings Report
feed.summary: Kimberly-Clark is facing intense investor scrutiny ahead of its January 27 quarterly earnings report, primarily due to concerns over the financial strain and integration complexities introduced by its $48.7 billion acquisition of Kenvue. With a heightened debt-to-equity ratio and cautious analyst outlooks, shareholders are closely watching for clarity on quarterly performance and concrete plans for debt reduction and integration milestones. The market's reaction will heavily depend on management's ability to address these concerns and provide a credible path forward.
